title: Midseason Invitational | Play-Ins
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Midseason Invitational | Play-Ins'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Midseason Invitational | Play-Ins  LoL Esports' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: T1 dominate play-in stage at Mid-Season Invitational
summary: T1 dominate play-in stage at Mid-Season Invitational  Field Level Media
content:
T1 swept all three matches in the play-in stage to advance to the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational bracket stage in Daejeon, South Korea.

T1 completed their flawless run with a 3-0 triumph on Wednesday against Team Liquid, prevailing in 28, 41 and 24 minutes.

T1 qualified for the bracket stage, an eight-team tournament that begins Friday with a $2 million prize pool, including $500,000 for the first-place finisher.

T1 will open the double-elimination bracket stage against Bilibili Gaming. The other Round 1 matches in the upper bracket feature LYON vs. FURIA, Hanwha Life vs. Secret Whales, and G2 Esports vs. Top Esports.

In the play-in final on Wednesday, T1 bot laner Soo-hwan “Peyz” Kim of South Korea claimed match MVP honors with a kills-deaths-assists ratio of 34-10-19.
